steps.title: Trace the underlined praise
steps.content: The underlined portion says that Grant praises Little, Big for blending the naive and sentimental modes. The explanation after the dash defines those modes, but the underlined phrase itself identifies what Grant admires about the novel.

steps.title: Separate the nearby details
steps.content: B points to the later clarification of the two modes, not to the underlined portion. C invents a comparison between Dempsey and Lowell, and D requires a contrast between two critics even though only Grant's opinion is presented. The answer is A.
